description:
At `Term::CallClosure(v, args, cont)` and `Term::TailCallClosure(v, args)` inside the typer's queried-set walk, when `fn_constants[v] = F` is known: register `(F, typed_args)` in the queried-set just like a direct call. This adds F's narrow spec for the typed args.

## Why split off the suppression

The original scope also suppressed F's any-key registration from the originating MakeClosure. Investigation during execution surfaced that suppression alone breaks codegen: typed closure stubs (.29.12.3) dispatch through F's any-key at the runtime closure header until the IR rewrite (.29.10.3) replaces CallClosure with a direct Call. Suppression and rewrite must land together. Moved to .29.10.3.

## Scope

- `src/ir_typer.rs` queried-set walk (the direct-call query loop): extend the `callee_id` match arm to include `Term::CallClosure { closure, .. }` and `Term::TailCallClosure { closure, .. }` when `ft.fn_constants[closure]` is set.
- No suppression yet; the originating MakeClosure still registers F's any-key.
- New helper `prim_var_uses(prim, &mut buf)` (used in .29.10.3 for the escape analysis).

## Tests

- `callclosure_with_fn_constant_registers_narrow_spec`: apply2(double, 21) → queried-set for double contains [int_lit(21)] (so a narrow spec exists alongside the any-key).
- Full suite + parity fixtures stay green (any-key still registered, codegen unchanged).
title: Typer registers narrow F-spec from known-target CallClosure
